NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Death of the Son.” A Psalm of David. I will praise You, O LORD, with my whole heart; I will tell of all Your marvelous works.
NKJV.2: I will be glad and rejoice in You; I will sing praise to Your name, O Most High.
NKJV.3: When my enemies turn back, They shall fall and perish at Your presence.
NKJV.4: For You have maintained my right and my cause; You sat on the throne judging in righteousness.
NKJV.5: You have rebuked the nations, You have destroyed the wicked; You have blotted out their name forever and ever.
NKJV.6: O enemy, destructions are finished forever! And you have destroyed cities; Even their memory has perished.
NKJV.7: But the LORD shall endure forever; He has prepared His throne for judgment.
NKJV.8: He shall judge the world in righteousness, And He shall administer judgment for the peoples in uprightness.
NKJV.9: The LORD also will be a refuge for the oppressed, A refuge in times of trouble.
NKJV.10: And those who know Your name will put their trust in You; For You, LORD, have not forsaken those who seek You.
NKJV.11: Sing praises to the LORD, who dwells in Zion!  Declare His deeds among the people.
NKJV.12: When He avenges blood, He remembers them; He does not forget the cry of the humble.
NKJV.13: Have mercy on me, O LORD! Consider my trouble from those who hate me, You who lift me up from the gates of death,
NKJV.14: That I may tell of all Your praise In the gates of the daughter of Zion. I will rejoice in Your salvation.
NKJV.15: The nations have sunk down in the pit which they made; In the net which they hid, their own foot is caught.
NKJV.16: The LORD is known by the judgment He executes; The wicked is snared in the work of his own hands. Meditation. Selah
NKJV.17: The wicked shall be turned into hell, And all the nations that forget God.
NKJV.18: For the needy shall not always be forgotten; The expectation of the poor shall not perish forever.
NKJV.19: Arise, O LORD, Do not let man prevail; Let the nations be judged in Your sight.
NKJV.20: Put them in fear, O LORD, That the nations may know themselves to be but men. Selah

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S> upon Muthlabben<S>4192</S> <S>1121</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. I will praise<S>3034</S> thee, O LORD<S>3068</S>, with my whole heart<S>3820</S>; I will shew forth<S>5608</S> all thy marvellous works<S>6381</S>.
KJV.2: I will be glad<S>8055</S> and rejoice<S>5970</S> in thee: I will sing praise<S>2167</S> to thy name<S>8034</S>, O thou most High<S>5945</S>.
KJV.3: When mine enemies<S>341</S> are turned<S>7725</S> back<S>268</S>, they shall fall<S>3782</S> and perish<S>6</S> at thy presence<S>6440</S>.
KJV.4: For thou hast maintained<S>6213</S> my right<S>4941</S> and my cause<S>1779</S>; thou satest<S>3427</S> in the throne<S>3678</S> judging<S>8199</S> right<S>6664</S>. <sup>maintained: Heb. made my judgment</sup> <sup>judging right: Heb. judging in righteousness</sup>

KJV.6: O thou enemy<S>341</S>, destructions<S>2723</S> are come to a perpetual<S>5331</S> end<S>8552</S>: and thou hast destroyed<S>5428</S> cities<S>6145</S> <S>5892</S>; their memorial<S>2143</S> is perished<S>6</S> with them<S>1992</S>. <sup>O thou: or, The destructions of the enemy are come to a perpetual end: and their cities hast thou destroyed, etc</sup>
KJV.7: But the LORD<S>3068</S> shall endure<S>3427</S> for ever<S>5769</S>: he hath prepared<S>3559</S> his throne<S>3678</S> for judgment<S>4941</S>.
KJV.8: And he shall judge<S>8199</S> the world<S>8398</S> in righteousness<S>6664</S>, he shall minister judgment<S>1777</S> to the people<S>3816</S> in uprightness<S>4339</S>.
KJV.9: The LORD<S>3068</S> also will be a refuge<S>4869</S> for the oppressed<S>1790</S>, a refuge<S>4869</S> in times<S>6256</S> of trouble<S>6869</S>. <sup>a refuge: Heb. an high place</sup>
KJV.10: And they that know<S>3045</S> thy name<S>8034</S> will put their trust<S>982</S> in thee: for thou, LORD<S>3068</S>, hast not forsaken<S>5800</S> them that seek<S>1875</S> thee.
KJV.11: Sing praises<S>2167</S> to the LORD<S>3068</S>, which dwelleth<S>3427</S> in Zion<S>6726</S>: declare<S>5046</S> among the people<S>5971</S> his doings<S>5949</S>.
KJV.12: When he maketh inquisition<S>1875</S> for blood<S>1818</S>, he remembereth<S>2142</S> them: he forgetteth<S>7911</S> not the cry<S>6818</S> of the humble<S>6035</S> <S>6041</S>. <sup>humble: or, afflicted</sup>
KJV.13: Have mercy<S>2603</S> upon me, O LORD<S>3068</S>; consider<S>7200</S> my trouble<S>6040</S> which I suffer of them that hate<S>8130</S> me, thou that liftest me up<S>7311</S> from the gates<S>8179</S> of death<S>4194</S>:
KJV.14: That I may shew forth<S>5608</S> all thy praise<S>8416</S> in the gates<S>8179</S> of the daughter<S>1323</S> of Zion<S>6726</S>: I will rejoice<S>1523</S> in thy salvation<S>3444</S>.
KJV.15: The heathen<S>1471</S> are sunk down<S>2883</S> in the pit<S>7845</S> that they made<S>6213</S>: in the net<S>7568</S> which<S>2098</S> they hid<S>2934</S> is their own foot<S>7272</S> taken<S>3920</S>.
KJV.16: The LORD<S>3068</S> is known<S>3045</S> by the judgment<S>4941</S> which he executeth<S>6213</S>: the wicked<S>7563</S> is snared<S>5367</S> in the work<S>6467</S> of his own hands<S>3709</S>. Higgaion<S>1902</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>. <sup>Higgaion: that is, Meditation</sup>
KJV.17: The wicked<S>7563</S> shall be turned<S>7725</S> into hell<S>7585</S>, and all the nations<S>1471</S> that forget<S>7913</S> God<S>430</S>.
KJV.18: For the needy<S>34</S> shall not alway<S>5331</S> be forgotten<S>7911</S>: the expectation<S>8615</S> of the poor<S>6041</S> <S>6035</S> shall not perish<S>6</S> for ever<S>5703</S>.
KJV.19: Arise<S>6965</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>; let not man<S>582</S> prevail<S>5810</S>: let the heathen<S>1471</S> be judged<S>8199</S> in thy sight<S>6440</S>.
KJV.20: Put<S>7896</S> them in fear<S>4172</S> <S>4172</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>: that the nations<S>1471</S> may know<S>3045</S> themselves to be but men<S>582</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>.

ESV.1: I will give thanks to the LORD with my whole heart; I will recount all of your wonderful deeds.
ESV.2: I will be glad and exult in you; I will sing praise to your name, O Most High.
ESV.3: When my enemies turn back, they stumble and perish before  your presence.
ESV.4: For you have maintained my just cause; you have sat on the throne, giving righteous judgment.
ESV.5: You have rebuked the nations; you have made the wicked perish; you have blotted out their name forever and ever.
ESV.6: The enemy came to an end in everlasting ruins; their cities you rooted out; the very memory of them has perished.
ESV.7: But the LORD sits enthroned forever; he has established his throne for justice,
ESV.8: and he judges the world with righteousness; he judges the peoples with uprightness.
ESV.9: The LORD is a stronghold for the oppressed, a stronghold in times of trouble.
ESV.10: And those who know your name put their trust in you, for you, O LORD, have not forsaken those who seek you.
ESV.11: Sing praises to the LORD, who sits enthroned in Zion! Tell among the peoples his deeds!
ESV.12: For he who avenges blood is mindful of them; he does not forget the cry of the afflicted.
ESV.13: Be gracious to me, O LORD! See my affliction from those who hate me, O you who lift me up from the gates of death,
ESV.14: that I may recount all your praises, that in the gates of the daughter of Zion I may rejoice in your salvation.
ESV.15: The nations have sunk in the pit that they made; in the net that they hid, their own foot has been caught.
ESV.16: The LORD has made himself known; he has executed judgment; the wicked are snared in the work of their own hands. Higgaion. Selah
ESV.17: The wicked shall return to Sheol, all the nations that forget God.
ESV.18: For the needy shall not always be forgotten, and the hope of the poor shall not perish forever.
ESV.19: Arise, O LORD! Let not man prevail; let the nations be judged before you!
ESV.20: Put them in fear, O LORD! Let the nations know that they are but men! Selah

NASB.1: For the choir director; on Muth-labben. A Psalm of David. I will give thanks to the LORD with all my heart; I will tell of all Your wonders.
NASB.2: I will be glad and exult in You; I will sing praise to Your name, O Most High.
NASB.3: When my enemies turn back, They stumble and perish before You.
NASB.4: For You have maintained my just cause; You have sat on the throne judging righteously.
NASB.5: You have rebuked the nations, You have destroyed the wicked; You have blotted out their name forever and ever.
NASB.6: The enemy has come to an end in perpetual ruins, And You have uprooted the cities; The very memory of them has perished.
NASB.7: But the LORD abides forever; He has established His throne for judgment,
NASB.8: And He will judge the world in righteousness; He will execute judgment for the peoples with equity.
NASB.9: The LORD also will be a stronghold for the oppressed, A stronghold in times of trouble;
NASB.10: And those who know Your name will put their trust in You, For You, O LORD, have not forsaken those who seek You.
NASB.11: Sing praises to the LORD, who dwells in Zion; Declare among the peoples His deeds.
NASB.12: For He who requires blood remembers them; He does not forget the cry of the afflicted.
NASB.13: Be gracious to me, O LORD; See my affliction from those who hate me, You who lift me up from the gates of death,
NASB.14: That I may tell of all Your praises, That in the gates of the daughter of Zion I may rejoice in Your salvation.
NASB.15: The nations have sunk down in the pit which they have made; In the net which they hid, their own foot has been caught.
NASB.16: The LORD has made Himself known; He has executed judgment. In the work of his own hands the wicked is snared. Higgaion Selah.
NASB.17: The wicked will return to Sheol, [Even] all the nations who forget God.
NASB.18: For the needy will not always be forgotten, Nor the hope of the afflicted perish forever.
NASB.19: Arise, O LORD, do not let man prevail; Let the nations be judged before You.
NASB.20: Put them in fear, O LORD; Let the nations know that they are but men. Selah.

NLT.1:
I will praise you, LORD, with all my heart;
I will tell of all the marvelous things you have done.
NLT.2:
I will be filled with joy because of you.
I will sing praises to your name, O Most High.
NLT.3:
My enemies retreated;
they staggered and died when you appeared.
NLT.4:
For you have judged in my favor;
from your throne you have judged with fairness.
NLT.5:
You have rebuked the nations and destroyed the wicked;
you have erased their names forever.
NLT.6:
The enemy is finished, in endless ruins;
the cities you uprooted are now forgotten.
NLT.7:
But the LORD reigns forever,
executing judgment from his throne.
NLT.8:
He will judge the world with justice
and rule the nations with fairness.
NLT.9:
The LORD is a shelter for the oppressed,
a refuge in times of trouble.
NLT.10:
Those who know your name trust in you,
for you, O LORD, do not abandon those who search for you.
NLT.11:
Sing praises to the LORD who reigns in Jerusalem.
Tell the world about his unforgettable deeds.
NLT.12:
For he who avenges murder cares for the helpless.
He does not ignore the cries of those who suffer.
NLT.13:
LORD, have mercy on me.
See how my enemies torment me.
Snatch me back from the jaws of death.
NLT.14:
Save me so I can praise you publicly at Jerusalem’s gates,
so I can rejoice that you have rescued me.
NLT.15:
The nations have fallen into the pit they dug for others.
Their own feet have been caught in the trap they set.
NLT.16:
The LORD is known for his justice.
The wicked are trapped by their own deeds. Quiet Interlude
NLT.17:
The wicked will go down to the grave.
This is the fate of all the nations who ignore God.
NLT.18:
But the needy will not be ignored forever;
the hopes of the poor will not always be crushed.
NLT.19:
Arise, O LORD!
Do not let mere mortals defy you!
Judge the nations!
NLT.20:
Make them tremble in fear, O LORD.
Let the nations know they are merely human. Interlude

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. Set to “The Death of the Son.” A Psalm by David. I will give thanks to Yahweh with my whole heart. I will tell of all your marvelous works.
WEB.2: I will be glad and rejoice in you. I will sing praise to your name, O Most High.
WEB.3: When my enemies turn back, they stumble and perish in your presence.
WEB.4: For you have maintained my just cause. You sit on the throne judging righteously.
WEB.5: You have rebuked the nations. You have destroyed the wicked. You have blotted out their name forever and ever.
WEB.6: The enemy is overtaken by endless ruin. The very memory of the cities which you have overthrown has perished.
WEB.7: But Yahweh reigns forever. He has prepared his throne for judgment.
WEB.8: He will judge the world in righteousness. He will administer judgment to the peoples in uprightness.
WEB.9: Yahweh will also be a high tower for the oppressed; a high tower in times of trouble.
WEB.10: Those who know your name will put their trust in you, for you, Yahweh, have not forsaken those who seek you.
WEB.11: Sing praises to Yahweh, who dwells in Zion, and declare among the people what he has done.
WEB.12: For he who avenges blood remembers them. He doesn’t forget the cry of the afflicted.
WEB.13: Have mercy on me, Yahweh. See my affliction by those who hate me, and lift me up from the gates of death;
WEB.14: that I may show all of your praise. In the gates of the daughter of Zion, I will rejoice in your salvation.
WEB.15: The nations have sunk down in the pit that they made. In the net which they hid, their own foot is taken.
WEB.16: Yahweh has made himself known. He has executed judgment. The wicked is snared by the work of his own hands. Meditation. Selah.
WEB.17: The wicked shall be turned back to Sheol,  even all the nations that forget God.
WEB.18: For the needy shall not always be forgotten, nor the hope of the poor perish forever.
WEB.19: Arise, Yahweh! Don’t let man prevail. Let the nations be judged in your sight.
WEB.20: Put them in fear, Yahweh. Let the nations know that they are only men. Selah.

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; on Muth-labben. A Psalm of David. I will give thanks and praise the LORD, with all my heart;
I will tell aloud all Your wonders and marvelous deeds.
AMP.2:
I will rejoice and exult in you;
I will sing praise to Your name, O Most High.
AMP.3:
When my enemies turn back,
They stumble and perish before You.
AMP.4:
For You have maintained my right and my cause;
You have sat on the throne judging righteously.
AMP.5:
You have rebuked the nations, You have destroyed the wicked and unrepentant;
You have wiped out their name forever and ever.
AMP.6:
The enemy has been cut off and has vanished in everlasting ruins,
You have uprooted their cities;
The very memory of them has perished.
AMP.7:
But the LORD will remain and sit enthroned forever;
He has prepared and established His throne for judgment.
AMP.8:
And He will judge the world in righteousness;
He will execute judgment for the nations with fairness (equity).
AMP.9:
The LORD also will be a refuge and a stronghold for the oppressed,
A refuge in times of trouble;
AMP.10:
And those who know Your name [who have experienced Your precious mercy] will put their confident trust in You,
For You, O LORD, have not abandoned those who seek You.
AMP.11:
Sing praises to the LORD, who dwells in Zion;
Declare among the peoples His [great and wondrous] deeds.
AMP.12:
For He who avenges blood [unjustly shed] remembers them (His people);
He does not forget the cry of the afflicted and abused.
AMP.13:
Have mercy on me and be gracious to me, O LORD;
See how I am afflicted by those who hate me,
You who lift me up from the gates of death,
AMP.14:
That I may tell aloud all Your praises,
That in the gates of the daughter of Zion (Jerusalem)
I may rejoice in Your salvation and Your help.
AMP.15:
The nations have sunk down in the pit which they have made;
In the net which they hid, their own foot has been caught.
AMP.16:
The LORD has made Himself known;
He executes judgment;
The wicked are trapped by the work of their own hands. Higgaion (meditation) Selah.
AMP.17:
The wicked will turn to Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead),
Even all the nations who forget God.
AMP.18:
For the poor will not always be forgotten,
Nor the hope of the burdened perish forever.
AMP.19:
Arise, O LORD, do not let man prevail;
Let the nations be judged before You.
AMP.20:
Put them in [reverent] fear of You, O LORD,
So that the nations may know they are but [frail and mortal] men. Selah.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
I'm thanking you, God, from a full heart,
I'm writing the book on your wonders.
MSG.2:
I'm whistling, laughing, and jumping for joy;
I'm singing your song, High God.
MSG.3:
The day my enemies turned tail and ran,
they stumbled on you and fell on their faces.
MSG.4:
You took over and set everything right;
when I needed you, you were there, taking charge.
MSG.5:
You blow the whistle on godless nations;
you throw dirty players out of the game,
wipe their names right off the roster.
MSG.6:
Enemies disappear from the sidelines,
their reputation trashed,
their names erased from the halls of fame.
MSG.7:
God holds the high center,
he sees and sets the world's mess right.
MSG.8:
He decides what is right for us earthlings,
gives people their just deserts.
MSG.9:
God's a safe-house for the battered,
a sanctuary during bad times.
MSG.10:
The moment you arrive, you relax;
you're never sorry you knocked.
MSG.11:
Sing your songs to Zion-dwelling God,
tell his stories to everyone you meet:
MSG.12:
How he tracks down killers
yet keeps his eye on us,
registers every whimper and moan.
MSG.13:
Be kind to me, God;
I've been kicked around long enough.
Once you've pulled me back
from the gates of death,
MSG.14:
I'll write the book on Hallelujahs;
on the corner of Main and First
I'll hold a street meeting;
I'll be the song leader; we'll fill the air
with salvation songs.
MSG.15:
They're trapped, those godless countries,
in the very snares they set,
Their feet all tangled
in the net they spread.
MSG.16:
They have no excuse;
the way God works is well-known.
The cunning machinery made by the wicked
has maimed their own hands.
MSG.17:
The wicked bought a one-way
ticket to hell.
MSG.18:
No longer will the poor be nameless—
no more humiliation for the humble.
MSG.19:
Up, God! Aren't you fed up with their empty strutting?
Expose these grand pretensions!
MSG.20:
Shake them up, God!
Show them how silly they look.

NIV.1:
Psalm 9
For the director of music. To the tune of “The Death of the Son.” A psalm of David.
I will praise you, O Lord, with all my heart;
I will tell of all your wonders.
NIV.2:
I will be glad and rejoice in you;
I will sing praise to your name, O Most High.
NIV.3:
My enemies turn back;
they stumble and perish before you.
NIV.4:
For you have upheld my right and my cause;
you have sat on your throne, judging righteously.
NIV.5:
You have rebuked the nations and destroyed the wicked;
you have blotted out their name for ever and ever.
NIV.6:
Endless ruin has overtaken the enemy,
you have uprooted their cities;
even the memory of them has perished.
NIV.7:
The Lord reigns forever;
he has established his throne for judgment.
NIV.8:
He will judge the world in righteousness;
he will govern the peoples with justice.
NIV.9:
The Lord is a refuge for the oppressed,
a stronghold in times of trouble.
NIV.10:
Those who know your name will trust in you,
for you, Lord, have never forsaken those who seek you.
NIV.11:
Sing praises to the Lord, enthroned in Zion;
proclaim among the nations what he has done.
NIV.12:
For he who avenges blood remembers;
he does not ignore the cry of the afflicted.
NIV.13:
O Lord, see how my enemies persecute me!
Have mercy and lift me up from the gates of death,
NIV.14:
that I may declare your praises
in the gates of the Daughter of Zion
and there rejoice in your salvation.
NIV.15:
The nations have fallen into the pit they have dug;
their feet are caught in the net they have hidden.
NIV.16:
The Lord is known by his justice;
the wicked are ensnared by the work of their hands. Higgaion. Selah
NIV.17:
The wicked return to the grave,
all the nations that forget God.
NIV.18:
But the needy will not always be forgotten,
nor the hope of the afflicted ever perish.
NIV.19:
Arise, O Lord, let not man triumph;
let the nations be judged in your presence.
NIV.20:
Strike them with terror, O Lord;
let the nations know they are but men. Selah

NRSVCE.1:
I will give thanks to the LORD with my whole heart;
I will tell of all your wonderful deeds.
NRSVCE.2:
I will be glad and exult in you;

When my enemies turned back,
they stumbled and perished before you.

For you have maintained my just cause;

You have rebuked the nations, you have destroyed the wicked;

But the LORD sits enthroned forever,

NRSVCE.12:
For he who avenges blood is mindful of them;
he does not forget the cry of the afflicted.
NRSVCE.13:
Be gracious to me, O LORD.
See what I suffer from those who hate me;
you are the one who lifts me up from the gates of death,
NRSVCE.14:
so that I may recount all your praises,
and, in the gates of daughter Zion,
rejoice in your deliverance.
NRSVCE.15:
The nations have sunk in the pit that they made;
in the net that they hid has their own foot been caught.
NRSVCE.16:
The LORD has made himself known, he has executed judgment;
the wicked are snared in the work of their own hands. Higgaion. Selah
NRSVCE.17:
The wicked shall depart to Sheol,
all the nations that forget God.
NRSVCE.18:
For the needy shall not always be forgotten,
nor the hope of the poor perish forever.
NRSVCE.19:
Rise up, O LORD! Do not let mortals prevail;
let the nations be judged before you.
NRSVCE.20:
Put them in fear, O LORD;
let the nations know that they are only human. Selah

NET.1: I will thank the LORD with all my heart! I will tell about all your amazing deeds!
NET.2: I will be happy and rejoice in you! I will sing praises to you, O sovereign One!
NET.3: When my enemies turn back, they trip and are defeated before you.
NET.4: For you defended my just cause; from your throne you pronounced a just decision.
NET.5: You terrified the nations with your battle cry; you destroyed the wicked; you permanently wiped out all memory of them.
NET.6: The enemy’s cities have been reduced to permanent ruins; you destroyed their cities; all memory of the enemies has perished.
NET.7: But the LORD rules forever; he reigns in a just manner.
NET.8: He judges the world fairly; he makes just legal decisions for the nations.
NET.9: Consequently the LORD provides safety for the oppressed; he provides safety in times of trouble.
NET.10: Your loyal followers trust in you, for you, LORD, do not abandon those who seek your help.
NET.11: Sing praises to the LORD, who rules in Zion! Tell the nations what he has done!
NET.12: For the one who takes revenge against murderers took notice of the oppressed; he did not overlook their cry for help
NET.13: when they prayed: “Have mercy on me, LORD! See how I am oppressed by those who hate me, O one who can snatch me away from the gates of death!
NET.14: Then I will tell about all your praiseworthy acts; in the gates of Daughter Zion I will rejoice because of your deliverance.”
NET.15: The nations fell into the pit they had made; their feet were caught in the net they had hidden.
NET.16: The LORD revealed himself; he accomplished justice; the wicked were ensnared by their own actions. (Higgaion. Selah)
NET.17: The wicked are turned back and sent to Sheol; this is the destiny of all the nations that ignore God,
NET.18: for the needy are not permanently ignored, the hopes of the oppressed are not forever dashed.
NET.19: Rise up, LORD! Don’t let men be defiant! May the nations be judged in your presence!
NET.20: Terrify them, LORD! Let the nations know they are mere mortals! (Selah)
